summary: Italian modernist architect, Pritzker Prize winner. Designed the Centre Pompidou in Paris (with Richard Rogers) — famous for its inside-out, color-coded exterior pipes and tubes, inspired by Archigram's Plug-in City and Cedric Price's Fun Palace. Also designed The Shard (London's tallest building, 95 stories, inspired by Canaletto), Kansai International Airport (on an artificial island in Osaka Bay), the Whitney Museum of American Art (New York), the Menil Collection (Houston), the Modern Wing of the Art Institute of Chicago (with the aluminum 'Flying Carpet' sunshade), and the Jean-Marie Tjibaou Cultural Centre in Noumea, New Caledonia.

works.name: Centre Pompidou (1977)
works.description: Co-designed with Richard Rogers (and engineer Peter Rice). A cultural complex in Paris featuring a modern art museum (French National Museum of Modern Art), a library, and IRCAM (a musical research center). Famous for its 'inside-out' design — elevators, electrical wiring, and plumbing are color-coded and displayed on the outside of the building, with external escalators in red across the facade. Inspired by Archigram's 'Plug-in City' and Cedric Price's Fun Palace. Named for former French President Georges Pompidou. The Stravinsky Fountain with 16 sculptures by Niki de Saint-Phalle and Jean Tinguely stands nearby. Gordon Matta-Clark made 'Conical Intersect' to critique the gentrification caused by its construction. Called a 'Trojan Horse' by Sanford Kwinter; 9-ton cast-steel 'gerberettes' support its trusses. Nam June Paik broadcast 'Good Morning, Mr. Orwell' from this building. Wikipedia

examples: collaborated with Richard Rogers on a complex in Paris with a distinctive inside-out look
a museum that has its elevators, electrical wiring, and plumbing in colored tubes on the outside
worked closely with Richard Rogers on a building noted for its use of multi-colored tubing

clues.clue: Named for a former President of France (Georges Pompidou)

clues.clue: Called a 'Trojan Horse' in Sanford Kwinter's book Requiem: For the City at the End of the Millennium; 9-ton cast-steel 'gerberettes' support its long trusses
examples: This structure is notoriously called a 'Trojan Horse' in Sanford Kwinter's book Requiem: For the City at the End of the Millennium. To help engineer this masterpiece, 9-ton, cast-steel 'gerberettes' were used to support its long trusses.

clues.clue: Largest modern art collection in Europe; adjacent to Stravinsky Place
examples: This cultural complex was home to the exhibit Matisse, Pairs and Sets during the second quarter of 2012, and it is located adjacent to the Stravinsky Place.

works.name: The Shard (2012)
works.description: A glass-clad pyramidal skyscraper in London — the tallest building in the United Kingdom and (at time of completion) the EU, standing 95 stories. Built on the site of the demolished Southwark Towers. Inspired by Canaletto's paintings of London's spires. Qatar owns a 95% share. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Tallest building in the United Kingdom (and formerly the EU); a glass-clad pyramidal skyscraper
examples: A glass-clad pyramidal skyscraper designed by this architect is the tallest building in the United Kingdom.
the tallest building in the European Union

clues.clue: Inspired by Canaletto's paintings of London's spires; the steep pyramidal shape was chosen for this reason
examples: Scenes by Canaletto depicting the spires of London inspired the steep pyramidal shape that this architect used in his design for the tallest building in the EU.

clues.clue: Built on the site of the demolished Southwark Towers
examples: The Southwark Towers were demolished to make room for one of this man's designs, the tallest building in the European Union.

clues.clue: 95 stories tall; Qatar owns a 95% share
examples: Qatar owns a 95-percent share in a Renzo Piano-designed pyramidal skyscraper in this city.
Renzo Piano designed a 95-story-tall pyramidal skyscraper in this city known as The Shard.

works.name: Kansai International Airport (1994)
works.description: An airport terminal in Osaka Bay, Japan, built on an artificial island (Kankujima) off the coast of Honshu. Features the world's longest airport terminal — a mile-long departure lounge. Wing-shaped; its sliding joints helped the terminal survive the 1995 Kobe earthquake unscathed. The building was inspired by the shape of an airfoil. Collaborator: Noriaki Okabe. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Built on an artificial island (Kankujima) in Osaka Bay, Japan, off the coast of Honshu
examples: For 10 points, name this Italian architect of Osaka's Kansai International Airport and The Shard.
an airport inspired by the shape of an airfoil on an artificial island in Osaka Bay, the Kansai International Airport

clues.clue: Features the world's longest airport terminal — a mile-long departure lounge
examples: A mile-long departure lounge is part of the world's longest airport terminal, which this architect designed on the artificial island of Kankujima.
This man's use of sliding joints helped a wing-shaped building survive the 1995 Kobe earthquake unscathed despite its mile-long departure lounge.

clues.clue: Sliding joints helped the wing-shaped building survive the 1995 Kobe earthquake unscathed
examples: This man's use of sliding joints helped a wing-shaped building survive the 1995 Kobe earthquake unscathed despite its mile-long departure lounge.

clues.clue: Designed with collaborator Noriaki Okabe
examples: This designer of the new Whitney Museum and the Shard collaborated with Noriaki Okabe on the longest airport terminal in the world.

works.name: Whitney Museum of American Art (2015)
works.description: A new building for the Whitney Museum in New York City's Meatpacking District, near the High Line. A nine-story building featuring a multi-level system of terraces and a cantilevered entrance leading to a 'largo.' Replaced a Marcel Breuer-designed building (originally on Madison Avenue). Wikipedia

clues.clue: Located at the southern entrance of the High Line park in the Meatpacking District; replaced a Marcel Breuer-designed building
examples: This architect replaced a Marcel Breuer-designed building in a Meatpacking District with a new terraced structure that opens to the High Line at the Whitney Museum.
He included a multi-level system of terraces in his design for a museum at the southern entrance of the High Line park.

clues.clue: Features a cantilevered entrance leading to a 'largo'; matched the industrial environment of the new location
examples: This architect designed a cantilevered entrance leading to a 'largo' in his design for the new Whitney Museum.
This architect matched the industrial environment of the new location in his design for the Whitney Museum's new building.

works.name: Jean-Marie Tjibaou Cultural Centre (1998)
works.description: A cultural center in Noumea, New Caledonia, built to celebrate Kanak culture. Features ten pavilions (called 'cases' with jutting ribs), divided into three 'villages' each based around a 'Great House' inspired by traditional Kanak hut architecture (featuring spires called fleches faitieres). Uses programmable glass louvers and slats of iroko wood imported from Africa, plus specially designed louvers called 'nacos' on the exterior to regulate wind flow. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Ten pavilions ('cases') inspired by traditional Kanak Great Houses; located in Noumea, New Caledonia
examples: This architect drew inspiration from traditional Kanak Great Houses for the ten-pavilion Jean-Marie Tjibaou Cultural Centre in Noumea.
This architect incorporated elements of Kanak hut architecture in his design for a convention center located in Noumea, New Caledonia.

clues.clue: Uses specially designed louvers called 'nacos' on the exterior to regulate wind flow
examples: Another work by this architect used specially designed louvers called 'nacos' on its exterior to regulate wind flow.

clues.clue: Divided into three 'villages'; features programmable glass louvers and iroko wood slats imported from Africa; cases have jutting ribs
examples: A set of buildings in this region feature programmable glass louvers and slats made from iroko wood imported from Africa, and are divided into three 'villages' each based around a 'Great House.'
'Cases' with jutting ribs make up this region's Jean-Marie Tjibaou Cultural Centre, one of the signature designs of Renzo Piano.

works.name: Menil Collection (1987)
works.description: A museum in Houston, Texas. Features ferroconcrete 'leaves' (also described as 'ferrous leaf' roofing) on the roof that serve as both a heat source and protection against UV light, maximizing natural light. Contains a Cy Twombly Pavilion. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Ferroconcrete 'leaves' (ferrous leaf roofing) on the roof serve as heat source and UV protection; houses the Cy Twombly Pavilion
examples: He used ferroconcrete leaves to serve as both a heat source and protection against UV light in the roof of a museum which houses his Cy Twombly Pavilion.
In this city, Renzo Piano used 'ferrous leaf' roofing to allow natural light to illuminate the Menil Collection of African and modern art.

works.name: Modern Wing, Art Institute of Chicago (2009)
works.description: An addition to the Art Institute of Chicago in Grant Park. Like many of Piano's museums, natural light is maximized in the design via an extended aluminum sunshade nicknamed 'The Flying Carpet.' Connected to Millennium Park by the Nichols Bridgeway. Wikipedia

images.caption: Modern Wing, Art Institute of Chicago (2009)

examples: Like many of his other museums, the use of natural light is maximized in this architect's design for the modern wing of the Art Institute of Chicago.
This man designed the Nichols Bridgeway to connect another of his creations to Millenium Park.

clues.clue: Features an extended aluminum sunshade nicknamed 'The Flying Carpet'
examples: Piano designed the Modern Wing of this museum in Grant Park with an extended sunshade made of aluminum that has been dubbed 'The Flying Carpet'.

works.name: Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum Expansion (2012)
works.description: Piano designed a visitor center for the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum in Boston, connected to the original building by Michael Van Valkenburgh's winding path through the Monk's Garden. The original building was inspired by the Palazzo Barbaro in Venice. The museum houses The Rape of Europa in its Titian Room, El Jaleo by John Singer Sargent, and was the site of the 1990 art theft that took Vermeer's The Concert and Rembrandt's The Storm on the Sea of Galilee. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Piano designed its visitor center; connected to the original building via Michael Van Valkenburgh's Monk's Garden winding path
examples: Michael Van Valkenburgh designed a winding path through the Monk's Garden that connects this museum's original building to its Renzo Piano-designed visitor center.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Renzo Piano is an Italian architect, winner of the Pritzker Prize. He is principally associated with Richard Rogers as his collaborator on the Centre Pompidou. His designs consistently maximize natural light in museums. He converted an automobile manufacturing plant into a convention center and trade fair district.

clues.clue: Italian architect, Pritzker Prize winner
examples: Name this Pritzker Prize-winning architect who co-designed the Pompidou Centre with Richard Rogers.

clues.clue: Consistent use of natural light in museum designs (a trademark)
examples: Like many of his other museums, the use of natural light is maximized in this architect's design for the modern wing of the Art Institute of Chicago.

clues.clue: Converted a gigantic automobile manufacturing plant into a convention center and trade fair district
examples: He converted a gigantic automobile manufacturing plant into a convention center and trade fair district.

comprehensive_summary:
Renzo Piano is an Italian architect and Pritzker Prize winner most famous in quizbowl for two works: the Centre Pompidou in Paris and The Shard in London. The Centre Pompidou was co-designed with Richard Rogers (Piano being the alphabetically-first designer) and is celebrated for its 'inside-out' aesthetic — elevators, electrical wiring, and plumbing are color-coded and displayed on the building's exterior, with external escalators in red across the facade. The design was inspired by Archigram's 'Plug-in City' and Cedric Price's Fun Palace. The complex contains the French National Museum of Modern Art, a public information library, and IRCAM (a musical research center). It was named for former French President Georges Pompidou. The nearby Stravinsky Fountain features 16 sculptures by Niki de Saint-Phalle and Jean Tinguely (including kinetic sculptures of a firebird and a fox). Architect-critic Sanford Kwinter called the building a 'Trojan Horse'; its engineering used 9-ton cast-steel 'gerberettes' to support its long trusses. Gordon Matta-Clark created 'Conical Intersect' to protest the gentrification caused by its construction, and Nam June Paik broadcast 'Good Morning, Mr. Orwell' from its studios. Rogers later went on to design the Lloyd's Building in London, while Piano designed The Shard — a 95-story glass-clad pyramidal skyscraper inspired by Canaletto's paintings of London's spires — which became the tallest building in the United Kingdom, built on the site of the demolished Southwark Towers. Qatar owns a 95% share in The Shard.

Piano also designed Kansai International Airport in Osaka, Japan, built on the artificial island of Kankujima in Osaka Bay, off the coast of Honshu. It features the world's longest airport terminal — a mile-long departure lounge — and a wing-shaped design whose sliding joints allowed it to survive the 1995 Kobe earthquake unscathed. His collaborator on this project was Noriaki Okabe. In New York, Piano designed the new Whitney Museum of American Art in the Meatpacking District, replacing a Marcel Breuer-designed building (originally on Madison Avenue) with a nine-story terraced structure opening onto the High Line, featuring a cantilevered entrance leading to a 'largo.'

In New Caledonia, Piano designed the Jean-Marie Tjibaou Cultural Centre in Noumea to celebrate Kanak culture — ten pavilions ('cases' with jutting ribs) divided into three 'villages,' each based around a 'Great House' inspired by traditional Kanak hut architecture (featuring spires called fleches faitieres). The building uses programmable glass louvers, iroko wood slats imported from Africa, and specially designed louvers called 'nacos' to regulate wind flow. His Menil Collection museum in Houston features ferroconcrete 'leaves' (also called 'ferrous leaf' roofing) that maximize natural light — a consistent Piano trademark — and contains a Cy Twombly Pavilion. The Modern Wing of the Art Institute of Chicago similarly maximizes natural light via an extended aluminum sunshade nicknamed 'The Flying Carpet,' and is connected to Millennium Park by the Nichols Bridgeway.

Piano also designed a visitor center for the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum in Boston, connected to the original building by a winding path through the Monk's Garden designed by landscape architect Michael Van Valkenburgh. Among Piano's other works: the Zentrum Paul Klee in Bern (wavy steel beams blending into surrounding hills; publishes Paul Klee's 'Writings on Form and Design Theory'); the California Academy of Sciences (green roof with dudleyas, inspired by San Francisco's seven hills); a renovation of Harvard's Fogg Museum that preserved the brick facade while adding a glass pyramid roof; an expansion of the Morgan Library with three glass-and-steel 'modernist entrance' pavilions added to the McKim Building; the Parliament House of Malta (Gozitan limestone, zero-emission design); the Kimbell Art Museum expansion in Fort Worth; the Biosfera glass-and-steel structure in Genoa's Porto Antico (housing 150 flora and fauna species); the Padre Pio Pilgrimage Church (Piano designed the attached convent with bare concrete walls, hidden light slit, concrete vaulted oratory, and orange-walled cells); the NEMO Science Museum in Amsterdam; Aurora Place in Sydney; and a redesign of Valletta City Gate in Malta, where he preserved the ruins of Edward Middleton Barry's Royal Opera House as an open-air space.
